figures.caption: Figure 2: AgentLens System Architecture. AgentLens uses Virtual Display to operate third-party apps in the background, while displaying the visual feedback (i.e., overlay) on the physical display.
Figure 3: Example screenshots of AgentLens using Full UI, Partial UI, and Gen UI visual interaction
Table 2: Task scenarios across four mobile applications.
Table C.1: Full agent screen flows per scenario. Bold [brackets] indicate the specific screens where an adaptive visual overlay was presented.
Table C.2: List of apps and the number of tasks for each.
Figure 4: (a) First-choice ranking for daily use. (b) Self-reported adoption intent for personal smartphone use (c) Preferred condition per app. (7-point Likert; error bars indicate ±1 SD).

Figure 6: Screenshot of each visual modalities under each user scenario in Formative Study 3: Full UI (complete original screen), Partial UI (task-relevant region extracted from the real app), and GenUI (LLM-generated interface), shown across six task scenarios.
